purpose: Renders a single agent task tile inside WavePipeline / ChainPipeline. Composed of a colored left border driven by status, top row (id badge, status dot, title), meta row (layer/directory/assigned_agent badges), and a status-dependent bottom row that shows summary+duration for completed, stage+ElapsedTimer+latest milestone for in_progress, fail text, or 'Waiting...' for pending. Also surfaces a deviation badge and a files-changed badge when applicable. Exports a sub-component StatusBadge used by WavePipeline/ChainPipeline headers, and a local ElapsedTimer that ticks 1Hz off `started_at`. Click handler suppresses navigation while text is selected.
gotchas: ElapsedTimer creates a setInterval per in_progress card — many simultaneous in_progress agents can mean many tickers. Effect cleanup is correct, but renders cascade through StatsBar's elapsed timer on the same cadence.

The component reads many optional fields from `agent` (stage, message, deviations, files_changed); these come from worker progress files merged with initialization.json — missing fields silently render nothing.
data-agent-id is load-bearing — WavePipeline's drawDependencyLines uses `[data-agent-id]` to look up DOM nodes for SVG line endpoints.
patterns: Status drives border, dot, and bg color via STATUS_COLORS / STATUS_BG_COLORS lookup with 'pending' fallback.
Inline style overrides allow per-status border/background while keeping all layout in CSS classes.
Click guard `window.getSelection().toString()` prevents accidental modal opens during text selection — also used in ChainPipeline cards.
conventions: Pure functional component, no internal state apart from ElapsedTimer's display string.
StatusBadge is a sibling export rather than a default to allow direct import in pipeline headers.
